reasoning: The market price on December 25, 2025, was 58%. As of early January 2026, Solana (SOL) is trading around $124–$130. To reach the $80 resolution threshold, SOL would need a further decline of approximately 35–38%. Over a 12-month horizon (until December 31, 2026), a 35% wick down or structural drawdown is historically common for large-cap cryptocurrencies, especially during corrective phases. SOL is currently down over 55% from its early 2025 all-time high of ~$294, facing institutional slowdowns and systemic vulnerabilities reported in late 2025. Given the high volatility of crypto markets, one full year provides ample opportunity for a flash crash or a continued bearish leg to touch $80. Therefore, I incrementally update the December market sentiment slightly upward due to sustained bearish price action and chart correction theories pointing toward lows below $100.
